tagline: ClickUp is a powerful project management tool. It was not built for agency economics.
intro: ClickUp is one of the most feature-rich project management tools available. For agencies, it delivers real value in the right context — and hits a structural ceiling in others. This comparison covers both, honestly.
agencyFloDesc: AgencyFlo is built specifically for agencies — combining project management, resource planning, financial visibility, client management, and FloAI in a single platform designed around agency workflows.
competitorDesc: ClickUp is a general-purpose project and task management platform used by teams across industries. It is highly configurable but requires significant setup to work for agency contexts.

pricing.agencyflo: Flat rate — no per-seat pricing. Early access pricing confirmed at launch.
pricing.competitor: Per-seat pricing from $7/user/month. Advanced features require higher tiers.

whoSuitsAgencyFlo: Agencies that need real-time financial visibility, resource planning, and client profitability — without building custom dashboards in a general-purpose tool.
whoSuitsCompetitor: Teams that need deep task customisation and are willing to configure the platform heavily. Works well for agencies that have a dedicated operations person to maintain it.

faqs.question: Can ClickUp track project margin?
faqs.answer: Not natively. You can track time and budget separately, but real-time margin per project requires custom fields, formulas, and significant configuration — or a separate spreadsheet.

faqs.question: Is AgencyFlo harder to set up than ClickUp?
faqs.answer: AgencyFlo is designed for agencies out of the box, so setup is faster. ClickUp requires considerable configuration to adapt its general-purpose structure to agency workflows.

faqs.question: Does ClickUp have an AI assistant?
faqs.answer: ClickUp has added some AI features, but they operate on task data rather than across the full agency — projects, clients, finances, and resources together. FloAI in AgencyFlo has full agency context.
